channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Structurally Driven, Reversible Topological Phase Transition in a Distorted Square Net Material
item.description:
Author(s): Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan

Topological materials hold immense promise for exhibiting exotic quantum phenomena, yet achieving controllable topological phase transitions remains challenging. Here, we demonstrate a structurally driven, reversible topological phase transition in the distorted square net material GdPS, induced via…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116603] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan

Topological materials hold immense promise for exhibiting exotic quantum phenomena, yet achieving controllable topological phase transitions remains challenging. Here, we demonstrate a structurally driven, reversible topological phase transition in the distorted square net material GdPS, induced via…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116603] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Structurally Driven, Reversible Topological Phase Transition in a Distorted Square Net Material
item.dc:creator: Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.description:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.dc:creator: Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.dc:creator: Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.dc:creator: Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.description:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.dc:creator: Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-$s$-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairing…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-s-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairings …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The $β$-pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$ is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two ${\mathrm{Ni}}^{2+}$ and two ${\mathrm{Cr}}^{3+}$ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The β-pyrochlore CsNiCrF6 is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two Ni2+ and two Cr3+ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutron scattering and numerical simulations, we show t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.dc:creator: Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.description:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a $d$-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermag…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a d-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.dc:creator: Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors ${\text{FeSe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ch}}_{x}$ ($\mathrm{Ch}=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Te}$) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors FeSe1−xChx (Ch=S,Te) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single-crystalline FeSe0.67Te0.33, positioned at the supercondu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.dc:creator: Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.description:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.dc:creator: Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.dc:creator: Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.dc:creator: Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu
